workflow.name: Manage Patreon and Ko-fi leads with Gmail, OpenAI and Google Sheets
workflow.description:
## Who is this for?
This is for creators who run Patreon and/or Kofi pages, support donations and want to automate their communication process.

## About
This system is listening for webhooks from patreon and kofi.
Once received, it detects the service by looking at headers. 

For Kofi it parses and validates the payload to make sure it's not fake. Once validated, it checks whether the person is on your newsletter lead list. If not, it adds person to your newsletter list. Then it checks the status of the payload, either it's a donation, subscription or shop purchase. 

For Patreon it checks whether the person is on your newsletter lead list and adds him if not. Then it checks the payload type using AI and returns a status, e.g. new patron, cancelled subscription etc.

## Customization
For Kofi you can customize emails that you'll be sending to members. For shop orders you can add invoice generation, supplier file generation, connect with your stock etc.
For Patreon you can customize emails you'll send to followers and patrons.
For both you can enable a crm where you'll track how much each person paid you so you know whether somebody upgrades or downgrades their subscription based on CRM data.

parameters.message: Someone just sent a fake payload to your Kofi webhook.
parameters.subject: fake payload detected!

values.content:
=You are classifying Patreon webhook events.

You are given:

1. Full webhook payload in `body.data`
2. An additional field called `event_type`, taken from webhook headers (`x-patreon-event`)

IMPORTANT RULES (STRICT):

* `event_type` is authoritative and OVERRIDES any inference from `body.data`.
* If `event_type == "members:delete"`, always return `subscription_cancelled`.
* If `event_type == "members:update"`, you must never return a `brand_new_*` status. Only return `follower_to_one_time`, `follower_to_subscription`, `subscription_upgraded`, `subscription_downgraded`, or `subscription_cancelled`.
* If `event_type == "members:create"`, you may return a `brand_new_*` status if the data matches.

Statuses (choose exactly one):

* brand_new_follower
* brand_new_patron_one_time
* brand_new_patron_subscription
* follower_to_one_time
* follower_to_subscription
* subscription_upgraded
* subscription_downgraded
* subscription_cancelled

How to classify:

1. subscription_cancelled

   * If `event_type == "members:delete"`, return this. Always.

2. Brand-new statuses (`brand_new_*`)

   * Only if `event_type == "members:create"`.
   * `brand_new_follower`: No prior membership, `patron_status` null or not a patron, no payment.
   * `brand_new_patron_one_time`: First-ever patron, `pledge_cadence == 0`.
   * `brand_new_patron_subscription`: First-ever patron, `pledge_cadence >= 1`.

3. Updates (`members:update`)

   * Never return any `brand_new_*` status.
   * `follower_to_one_time`: Previously a follower, now `pledge_cadence == 0`.
   * `follower_to_subscription`: Previously a follower, now `pledge_cadence >= 1`.
   * `subscription_upgraded`: Existing subscription, payment/tier increased.
   * `subscription_downgraded`: Existing subscription, payment/tier decreased.

Output format (STRICT):

```
{
  "status": "<one_of_the_statuses_above>"
}
```

---

This version is simplified, fully respects `event_type`, and prevents the LLM from incorrectly marking updates as "brand new".

parameters.content:
## Patreon & Ko-fi Webhook Processor

### About
This system is listening for webhooks from patreon and kofi.
Once received, it detects the service by looking at headers. 

For Kofi it parses and validates the payload to make sure it's not fake. Once validated, it checks whether the person is on your newsletter lead list. If not, it adds person to your newsletter list. Then it checks the status of the payload, either it's a donation, subscription or shop purchase. 

For Patreon it checks whether the person is on your newsletter lead list and adds him if not. Then it checks the payload type using AI and returns a status, e.g. new patron, cancelled subscription etc.

### Setup
- [ ] Connect your Gmail account used to send thank-you and alert messages
- [ ] Connect Google Sheets and select the Newsletter spreadsheet and sheet
- [ ] Add your OpenAI API key and select the desired model for classification
- [ ] Set the Ko-fi verification token in the workflow and Ko-fi webhook settings
- [ ] Configure your Patreon webhook to send payloads to the webhook URL and forward x-patreon-event. Configure 6 events that are not deprecated and are not post-related.
- [ ] Customize emails you'll be sending for each branch

### Customization
For Kofi you can customize emails that you'll be sending to members. For shop orders you can add invoice generation, supplier file generation, connect with your stock etc.
For Patreon you can customize emails you'll send to followers and patrons.
For both you can enable a crm where you'll track how much each person paid you so you know whether somebody upgrades or downgrades their subscription based on CRM data.

## Set Token
Get an auth token from Kofi to validate your payloads.

## Newsletter
We check whether person is on our newsletter list or not. If not, we add him. If not, skip.
Create a "Newsletter" spreadsheet with columns "email" and "name". Set up the node and feel free to add more columns if needed.

## Letters
Set up letters that you're gonna send to members.

## Newsletter
We check whether person is on our newsletter list or not. If not, we add him. If not, skip.
Create a "Newsletter" spreadsheet with columns "email" and "name". Set up the node and feel free to add more columns if needed.

## Status Validation
We use OpenAI to determine the status of operations

## Letters
Set up letters that you're gonna send to members.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
